title: Student murder: Accused granted bail
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The 21-year-old Nelson Mandela University (NMU) student Fandiswa Mthonti was granted bail in the amount of R1 000 with strict bail conditions, in the George Magistrate's Court today, Wednesday 4 December.

She has been placed in the care of her mother and uncle.


> Mthonti, represented by  Jerome Bans from Bulelani Bans Attorneys Inc, is charged with the murder of a fellow student, Lilitha Booi (23), in Le Vallia on Sunday 17 November.


The incident occurred at their student residence in Mountainview Crescent.

When police arrived at the scene around 19:00, they discovered the victim with multiple stab wounds.

According to available information, the victim was stabbed six times with a knife. Mthonti was apprehended at the scene. The motive for the incident is still under investigation.

Booi was a third-year tourism student at NMU.

Previous articles:

- Student murder: bail application next week
- Student's bail application next month
- Student in court for murder
- Murder at student residence in George

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
